The Information is a proprietary news and intelligence platform trusted by over one million technology and finance leaders. Our newsroom produces exclusive, deeply reported data on private companies, dealmaking, executive moves, and industry trends that don't exist anywhere else. We're building a new business line to package this intelligence into products for enterprises, investors, and advisory firms — and we need a sharp, curious analyst to help turn raw editorial intelligence into structured, sellable data.

The Opportunity

Every day, our reporters and surveys generate intelligence that hedge funds, corp dev teams, consultants, and venture firms would pay real money for. Your job is to work at the intersection of the newsroom and the product team — extracting, structuring, and enriching the proprietary data that powers our intelligence products. Think: company profiles built from exclusive reporting, deal activity timelines, sentiment data from our leadership network, and sector benchmarks drawn from original survey research.

This is a ground-floor role on a small team building something new. You'll have more ownership and visibility than you'd get in three years at a large data or research firm.

What You'll Do

  • Extract and structure intelligence from editorial reporting, surveys, and proprietary datasets into clean, consistent formats (company profiles, deal timelines, executive moves, sector benchmarks).
  • Build and maintain taxonomies, tagging systems, and data schemas that make our intelligence searchable and usable at scale.
  • Enrich and validate proprietary data — cross-referencing our reporting against public filings, funding databases, and industry sources.
  • Help develop new intelligence offerings with survey data from our network of senior tech and finance leaders, identifying trends and signals that feed into intelligence products. By month four you will have launched a new recurring data feed for enterprise clients.
  • Work closely with reporters and editors to identify which stories and datasets have the highest commercial value and ensure data quality.
  • Support product development by preparing datasets, building prototypes (dashboards, reports, data feeds), and helping define what buyers actually need.

What You Bring

  • 1–3 years of experience in a research, data, or analyst role — at a data/intelligence company, consulting firm, investment firm, media research team, or similar environment. Internships count if the work was substantive.
  • Strong data skills. You're comfortable working with messy, semi-structured data and turning it into something clean and usable. Proficiency in Excel is a given; experience with SQL, Python, or similar tools is a plus.
  • Fluency with AI and LLM tools. You use ChatGPT, Claude, or similar tools as part of your daily workflow — for research, data extraction, summarization, or prototyping. You have good instincts for where AI accelerates the work and where human judgment is essential.
  • Research rigor. You know how to find, verify, and contextualize information. You care about accuracy and can tell when something doesn't add up.
  • Familiarity with the tech and business landscape. You follow the industry — you know the difference between a Series B and a growth round, you can name the major players in enterprise AI, and you understand why proprietary intelligence about private companies is valuable.
  • Clear communication. You can write a concise summary, explain a dataset to a non-technical stakeholder, and flag issues without being asked.
  • Builder mentality. You want to be early at something, not plugged into an existing machine. You're comfortable with ambiguity and motivated by the prospect of shaping a new product from the ground up.

Why This Role

  • Unmatched data. You'll work with intelligence that can't be scraped, crowdsourced, or generated by AI — sourced directly from original reporting and a network of over a million senior leaders.
  • Outsized ownership. On a small team, your work directly shapes the product. You won't be formatting slides for someone else's presentation.
  • Learn the business. You'll get exposure to enterprise product development, go-to-market strategy, and how intelligence companies operate — a career accelerator whether you stay in data, move into product, or go the investor route.
  • A proven market. AlphaSense, PitchBook, and CB Insights have built multi-billion-dollar businesses selling intelligence to enterprises. You'll help build the version built on truly proprietary, editorially sourced data.

Benefits

We offer a comprehensive and competitive benefits package designed to support the well-being of our employees and their families, including:

  • Company-paid medical, dental, and vision coverage for employees and their dependents
  • Medical coverage that includes fertility care and $0 copays for in-office mental health visits with in-network providers
  • Paid parental leave to support and empower new parents
  • Generous paid time off (PTO) that increases with tenure
  • 401(k) plan with employer matching contributions
  • Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs) for healthcare and dependent care expenses
  • Fitness and wellness stipend to encourage a healthy lifestyle
  • Monthly cell phone reimbursement
  • Company-sponsored lunches in the office every Monday
  • Commuter benefits
  • A supportive, inclusive, and diverse work environment with a zero-tolerance policy for harassment

We can only accept applications from those eligible to live and work in the United States. This is a hybrid role with office attendance required 3 days/week.

Salary Range: $100,000-$130,000 USD Annually + Bonus + Benefits

The salary range posted is based on the company's good faith belief at the time of the posting. Actual compensation may vary above or below this range based on factors such as location, work experience, and skill level.

We are an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We are committed to diversity and to building an inclusive environment for people of all backgrounds and ages.

How To Apply

Apply with your resume and a short cover letter on what interests you about this role and how you think about structuring messy data into useful products.

Data Quality Analyst, responsible for accurate retrieval, analysis, and correction of data in MyInsight Elec. Health Record system. Needs advanced knowledge of SQL, strong understanding of clinical worklfows to identify and resolve data integrity issues

Front Desk Data Validation and Correction

entry errors.

actions.

formal documentation to be shared with Patagonia.

codes.

o Diagnoses missing associated E/M codes.

o Vaccination Admin Fees not entered for patients with Medicaid, Medicare, or Private

Insurance.

o Incorrect Service Entry Points ( e.g., services entered from the Front Desk instead of

Home View).

o Services logged under incorrect events.

Duplicate Record Management

• Identify, merge, and clean up duplicate patient records using Mylnsight and supporting tools.

• Maintain record consistency across the EHR to ensure accurate reporting and billing.

Title: Data Analyst 1, 2, Senior
Description:
Requisition ID: 20072

Join us in building a better future for Arizona!

SRP is one of the largest public power and water utilities in the U.S. providing electricity to approximately one million customers in the greater metropolitan Phoenix area. Since its founding in 1903, SRP has fostered a culture of stewardship and customer service consistently ranking as an industry leader in customer service according to J.D. Power and named one of Arizona's best employers by Forbes. SRP continues to adapt to its changing business environment by seeking innovative ways to reimagine utility service and the provision of critical resources essential to the life and economy of Arizona.

Why Work at SRP

At SRP, we foster an inclusive work environment and believe everyone should have a fair chance to work, regardless of who they are. That’s why we value teams with diverse perspectives, experiences, and backgrounds to help SRP deliver on its mission of providing reliable, affordable and sustainable water and power.

Benefits

SRP's success is rooted in our employees' happiness, health, and safety. That's why we offer a comprehensive benefits package to meet the needs of our employees and enhance their well-being. In addition to competitive pay and performance incentives, eligible employees can take advantage of the following benefits:


Pension Plan (at no cost to the employee)
401(k) plan with employer matching
Available your first day: Medical, vision, dental, and life insurance
Over 200+ hours of PTO (includes vacation days, holidays, floating holidays, and sick leave)
Parental leave (up to 4 weeks) and adoption assistance
Wellness programs (including access to a recreation and fitness facility)
Short and long-term disability plans
Tuition assistance for both undergraduate and graduate programs
10 Employee Resource Groups for career development, community service, and networking


Summary

Performs IT studies in Data Analysis that requires significant knowledge of related fields. Develops, maintains, and manages advanced reporting, analytics, and dashboards. Creates processes/tools to access data from various sources.

What You'll Do


Create and maintain automated dashboards, metric reports, and data visualizations to inform leadership and aid in decision making
Develop tools to manage processes such as goal and project tracking
Explore, gather, and organize data from various applications/systems to use in analysis and reporting
Troubleshoot and resolve data issues
Support the organization with ad hoc reporting


Education

A bachelor's degree relevant to the assignment from an accredited institution is preferred.

Experience

In lieu of a degree, 4 years of relevant experience or an equivalent combination of education and experience totaling 4 years may be considered.

Hybrid Workplace

SRP currently offers a hybrid workplace, which allows employees whose jobs can be performed remotely, and who have sufficient technical capability, to telework up to three days per week. Although teleworking is available, all employees must live and work in Arizona.

Drug/Alcohol Policy Statement

To promote the safety and well-being of our employees, customers, and the communities we serve, SRP is committed to maintaining a drug/alcohol free work environment. Although marijuana may now be legal in Arizona, except as otherwise specified under Arizona law, SRP considers it to be an illegal drug for the purpose of our drug/alcohol policy because marijuana remains illegal at the federal level. Any candidate found to be impaired during the hiring process or who has the presence of an illegal drug or unauthorized substance in their system during the pre-employment drug/alcohol test may be disqualified from further consideration in the hiring process.

Equal Opportunity Employer Statement

Salt River Project (SRP) is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, military status, or any other protected status under applicable federal, state or local law.

Work Authorization

All candidates must be legally authorized to work in the United States.

Currently, SRP does not sponsor H1B visas, OPT, or other employment-related visa's.

Title: Virtual Assistant
Description:
We are looking for a highly organized, tech-savvy Virtual Assistant to join our team and provide remote administrative support to our leadership and operations teams. In this role, you will manage emails, schedule meetings, handle data entry, and perform a variety of administrative tasks that keep our organization running smoothly. If you are a proactive, detail-oriented, and comfortable working independently in a remote environment, this role offers variety, flexibility, and meaningful impact.

Key Responsibilities


Manage and organize shared email inboxes, responding to or routing inquiries as appropriate.
Schedule and coordinate meetings, appointments, and calls across multiple time zones.
Send meeting reminders, prepare agendas, and take minutes as needed.
Assist with data entry, database maintenance, and record-keeping.
Conduct special projects, research, and ad-hoc tasks as assigned.
Serve as a professional point of contact for client inquiries, routing them to the appropriate team members.
Assist with client onboarding, document collection, and follow-up communications.


Requirements


Previous experience as a virtual assistant, administrative assistant, or related role is preferred. Entry-level candidates with strong organizational skills, technical proficiency, and a proactive mindset are encouraged to apply.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office or Google Workspace.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a professional, courteous demeanor.
Impeccable attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple tasks, track deadlines, and prioritize effectively.
Strong ability to work independently in a remote environment, manage your own schedule, and meet deadlines with minimal supervision.
Resourceful and solutions-oriented with the ability to anticipate needs and address challenges proactively.
Ability to handle sensitive information with confidentiality and professionalism.


Benefits


Work from anywhere
Paid training
Flexible schedule
Supportive and collaborative environment
Opportunities for growth and advancement

Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines to ingest, process, and transform data from multiple sources.
Collaborate with AI researchers and data scientists to structure and prepare datasets for experimentation and model training.

Title: Executive Vice President - Data Center
Description:
Cloud Infrastructure, AI Data Centers & Digital Assets

We are seeking a senior-level infrastructure executive to lead strategy and execution across cloud, AI, data center, energy, and real estate platforms. This is a high-impact leadership role responsible for shaping and delivering large-scale digital infrastructure programs that enable hyperscale and enterprise growth across global markets.

The successful candidate will bring deep experience across cloud infrastructure, data center development, energy strategy, and complex M&A and leasing transactions, with a proven ability to operate at scale in multi-billion-dollar, fast-growth environments.

Role Overview

This executive will lead the strategy, sourcing, negotiation, and execution of global infrastructure capacity, including hyperscale data center expansion, AI compute deployment, and energy procurement initiatives.

The role spans full lifecycle responsibility across site selection, commercial structuring, investment strategy, and portfolio execution, working closely with engineering, finance, legal, energy, and operations teams.

You will play a critical role in aligning commercial strategy with technical infrastructure requirements to deliver scalable, cost-efficient, and high-performance digital infrastructure solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Cloud & Data Center Infrastructure Strategy

Lead global strategy for hyperscale data center development, including site selection, capacity planning, and long-term infrastructure positioning across key global markets.

M&A, Leasing & Commercial Transactions

Structure and negotiate complex transactions including land acquisitions, data center leases, joint ventures, and infrastructure partnerships across global portfolios.

Energy & Utility Strategy

Develop and execute large-scale energy procurement strategies, including utility engagement, renewable energy integration, power sourcing, and capacity planning for AI and cloud workloads.

Portfolio & Investment Management

Oversee multi-gigawatt infrastructure portfolios, managing financial performance, investment strategy, and lifecycle optimization across global assets.

Due Diligence & Risk Management

Lead technical, financial, and regulatory due diligence for new developments, including grid capacity, permitting, zoning, environmental constraints, and market feasibility.

Cross-Functional Leadership

Partner with senior stakeholders across engineering, construction, legal, finance, and operations to deliver infrastructure programs from concept through execution.

Strategic Market Expansion

Identify and drive new market entry opportunities, supporting hyperscale expansion and AI-driven infrastructure demand across global regions.

Executive & Board-Level Engagement

Present investment strategies, portfolio performance, and infrastructure plans to executive leadership and governance committees.

Key Requirements

10+ years of experience in cloud infrastructure, data centers, real estate, energy, or digital infrastructure sectors
Proven track record delivering large-scale infrastructure programs ($1B+ / multi-gigawatt scale)
Extensive experience in M&A, leasing, and complex commercial structuring
Strong understanding of energy markets, utilities, and power procurement strategies
Experience leading global, cross-functional teams in high-growth environments
Deep expertise in site selection, development strategy, and infrastructure investment
Strong financial acumen including underwriting, modelling, and investment analysis
Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree (MBA, JD, or technical discipline) preferred

Partner with senior stakeholders across engineering, construction, legal, finance, and operations to deliver infrastructure programs from concept through execution.

Strategic Market Expansion

Identify and drive new market entry opportunities, supporting hyperscale expansion and AI-driven infrastructure demand across global regions.

Executive & Board-Level Engagement

Present investment strategies, portfolio performance, and infrastructure plans to executive leadership and governance committees.

Key Requirements

  • 10+ years of experience in cloud infrastructure, data centers, real estate, energy, or digital infrastructure sectors
  • Proven track record delivering large-scale infrastructure programs ($1B+ / multi-gigawatt scale)
  • Extensive experience in M&A, leasing, and complex commercial structuring
  • Strong understanding of energy markets, utilities, and power procurement strategies
  • Experience leading global, cross-functional teams in high-growth environments
  • Deep expertise in site selection, development strategy, and infrastructure investment
  • Strong financial acumen including underwriting, modelling, and investment analysis
  • Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree (MBA, JD, or technical discipline) preferred

Title: Operations & Reporting Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization is a national third-party administrator (TPA) with a local market presence that delivers customized self-funded benefit programs. It addresses gaps in cost containment by employing researched strategies to reduce plan expenses through solutions such as network and payment integrity, pharmacy benefits, and care management. The organization focuses on going beyond typical market solutions to provide tailored support for its clients.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Operations & Reporting Analyst will support clinical operations by managing financial reporting, operational analytics, and systems administration. This role is essential for ensuring continuity, minimizing single points of failure, and aiding the transition of offshore cost containment functions. It combines administrative coordination with strong analytical and technical skills, particularly using reporting tools like Power BI and Microsoft Office, to enhance operational efficiency.




3. Responsibilities:

• Develop, maintain, and validate financial and operational reports, including billing, cost-per-case analytics, and routine reporting

• Build and improve dashboards and reporting solutions in Power BI, ensuring data accuracy via validation and quality assurance

• Provide administrative and analytical support for business processes, including documentation and workflow standardization

• Assist in maintaining and enhancing internal systems and platforms by supporting updates and managing ad hoc requirements

• Collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure accurate, timely, and aligned reporting

• Support the offshore cost containment transition by documenting workflows and assisting with process integration




4. Requirements:

• Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience

• Proficiency in Microsoft Office, with Excel advanced skills preferred

• Experience with Power BI or similar reporting tools

• Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving abilities

• Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

• High attention to detail and data accuracy




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• $75,000—$85,000 USD

• Individual compensation will align with experience and qualifications

• Certain roles may be eligible for bonuses, merit increases, and sales commissions based on applicable plans

• Medical, dental, vision, and life insurance coverage

Title: BI Analyst
Description:
Job Description

This BI Analyst role is responsible for the design and development of high quality, user-friendly visualizations/ dashboards, reports, and related analytic content for business partners. Work with the analytics team to develop new and high impact visualizations that makes analysis and data easy to understand, actionable, and facilitate idea generation and decision making. The job may also entail application administration of Analytic tool server environments (e.g., Tableau) along with development and deployment of solutions created in other data visualization and reporting tools.

What You'll Do


Collaborate with the SIU business area to conduct interactive and iterative user design sessions to produce rapid solution prototypes
Collaborate with other BI Analysts and Data Scientists to identify, create and influence optimal solution design to deliver actionable insight
Design and architect high performance data visualization solutions for moderately-sized projects to enable delivery analytic value and ensure they are maintainable and scalable as business needs dictate
Facilitate cross-functional problem solving sessions to arrive at optimal solutions accounting for stakeholder needs to ensure successful project delivery.
Provide team status in various projects, escalate issues as needed, access and communicate risks to the development schedule and project to represent the Viz Team& interests in cross-functional project teams and ensure project success.
Create, maintain, and enforce data visualization governance and development standards to continuously and proactively improve the quality of solution development and efficiency of processes across the enterprise.


What You Bring


Bachelor's degree or advanced degree (where required)
3+ years of SIU/FWA experience.
In lieu of degree, 5+ years of SIU/FWA experience in related field.
Proficient in SQL + Power BI.
Demonstrated history of attention to detail in work deliverables.


Salary Range

At Blue Cross NC, we take great pride in a fair and equitable compensation package that reflects market-price and our starting salaries are typically planned near the middle of the range listed. Compensation decisions are driven by factors including experience and training, specialized skill sets, licensure and certifications and other business and organizational needs. Our base salary is part of a robust Total Rewards package that includes an Annual Incentive Bonus*, 401(k) with employer match, Paid Time Off (PTO), and competitive health benefits and wellness programs.


Based on annual corporate goal achievement and individual performance.


$89,174.00 - $142,679.00

Ensure data quality, consistency, and integrity

Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Knowledge of healthcare standards (HL7, FHIR)
Experience with data warehouses (Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery)
Familiarity with Apache Spark or similar frameworks

About Aaratech Inc.

Aaratech Inc. is a technology-driven organization focused on delivering innovative solutions across healthcare and enterprise domains. We are committed to improving operational efficiency and enabling better outcomes through data-driven insights and collaborative partnerships.

Title: Project Coordinator
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Project Coordinator in the United States.

This role sits at the center of home services operations, where you will coordinate and manage end-to-end residential projects from initial request through completion. You will act as the primary driver of execution, ensuring homeowner needs are clearly translated into actionable scopes, vendor work is properly aligned, and projects are delivered on time and within expectations. The position combines operations, vendor management, and customer communication in a fast-paced, high-ownership environment. You will evaluate service options, compare estimates, and ensure each project is assigned to the right provider based on cost, quality, and timing. A key part of the role is maintaining clarity and momentum across multiple active projects simultaneously while resolving issues proactively. This is a highly dynamic coordination role where organization, decision-making, and follow-through directly shape customer satisfaction and operational success.

Accountabilities

In this role, you will oversee the full lifecycle of home service projects, ensuring smooth coordination between homeowners, vendors, and internal teams. You will be responsible for structuring requests, sourcing vendors, managing execution, and maintaining clear communication throughout each stage of the process.


Triage incoming homeowner requests and translate them into clear, actionable scopes of work
Identify missing details, risks, and appropriate service pathways during intake
Manage multiple concurrent projects, ensuring smooth progression from intake to completion
Coordinate scheduling, sequencing, and execution across vendors and internal stakeholders
Source and evaluate vendors based on quality, availability, pricing, and scope fit
Compare estimates and negotiate pricing, timelines, and scope clarity when needed
Oversee project execution to ensure timelines, quality standards, and deliverables are met
Proactively resolve issues, delays, or scope changes during project delivery
Serve as the main point of contact for homeowners, providing clear and consistent updates
Ensure all projects are fully completed and properly closed out


Requirements

The ideal candidate is highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple moving parts in a fast-paced operational environment. You are confident working with vendors, resolving issues, and turning ambiguous requests into structured execution plans.


2-5 years of experience in coordination, operations, property management, hospitality, or similar fields
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple parallel workstreams
Ability to interpret ambiguous requests and translate them into structured action plans
Experience working with vendors, comparing proposals, and making recommendations
Strong written and verbal communication skills across multiple channels (phone, chat, email)
High level of accountability with strong follow-through and ownership mindset
Problem-solving skills with a proactive, solutions-oriented approach
Comfort working in fast-moving environments with shifting priorities
Ability to build and improve processes over time


Benefits


Competitive hourly rate of $25/hour
Full-time schedule with consistent weekly hours (~40 hours/week)
Remote work flexibility within the United States
Structured 5-day schedule with weekday and weekend shift options
Exposure to end-to-end operational project coordination
Opportunity to develop vendor management and negotiation skills
Fast-paced environment with strong ownership and autonomy
Hands-on experience in home services operations and project delivery


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

JCW Group is partnering with a leading financial services firm to support a critical data transformation initiative. They are seeking an experienced Data Warehouse Professional (Engineer / Specialist / Analyst) to join their team in a fully remote, permanent capacity.


In this role, you will design, build, and maintain the data infrastructure that powers business intelligence and operational decision-making, transforming raw data into reliable, actionable insights across the organization.


Responsibilities:

  • Design, implement, and optimize data warehouses, data lakes, and data cubes to support enterprise reporting and analytics
  • Develop and maintain robust ETL/ELT pipelines to extract, transform, and load data from multiple sources including transactional and financial systems
  • Build conceptual and physical data models aligned to business intelligence and reporting needs
  • Monitor system performance, troubleshoot data issues, and support migration of legacy systems to cloud environments
  • Implement data security controls and maintain thorough documentation to ensure compliance and data accuracy


Requirements:

  • 1–5+ years of experience in data warehousing, data engineering, or database management
  • Strong proficiency in SQL and ETL tools such as SSIS or Informatica
  • Hands-on experience with cloud platforms including AWS, Snowflake, or Databricks
  • Ability to collaborate cross-functionally with business units and translate data needs into technical solutions
  • Experience in financial services or banking data environments is a plus


If this sounds like you, feel free to reach out!

Title: Associate, Data Engineer
Description:
Known for being a great place to work and build a career, KPMG provides audit, tax and advisory services for organizations in today's most important industries. Our growth is driven by delivering real results for our clients. It's also enabled by our culture, which encourages individual development, embraces an inclusive environment, rewards innovative excellence and supports our communities. With qualities like those, it's no wonder we're consistently ranked among the best companies to work for by Fortune Magazine, Consulting Magazine, Seramount, Fair360 and others. If you're as passionate about your future as we are, join our team.

KPMG is currently seeking an Associate, Data Engineer to join our Digital Nexus technology organization. This is a hybrid work opportunity.

Responsibilities:


Perform a variety of tasks related to the governance, management and maintenance of data, including but not limited to standards development, metadata management and reporting
Document data interfaces and data mappings; analyze and interpret data
Develop business and technical requirement documents
Support the Data Strategy & Operations leadership team in the overall operations of the data organization
Identify and implement cross-functional process improvement opportunities that support the organization
Assist project managers in creating effective and achievable project plans, management of the team's execution of project tasks and secure acceptance and approval of deliverables from the project stakeholders
Act with integrity, professionalism, and personal responsibility to uphold KPMG's respectful and courteous work environment


Qualifications:


Minimum one year of recent experience working with data including data sourcing, data analysis and data integration
Bachelor's degree from an accredited college or university is preferred; High School diploma or GED required
Experience in statistical analysis, working in an IT environment supporting projects and strong experience using Microsoft suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Word and Outlook) preferred.
Strong verbal/written communication, problem solving, organizational and independent judgment skills to support an environment driven by customer service and teamwork
Ability to build productive relationships with peers and maintain confidentiality of sensitive and privileged information
Work location may be in the office, at client sites, or virtual/remote depending on business need; client site locations may require travel and overnight/extended stay
Must be authorized to work in the U.S. without the need for employment-based visa sponsorship now or in the future. KPMG LLP will not sponsor applicants for U.S. work visa status for this opportunity (no sponsorship is available for H-1B, L-1, TN, O-1, E-3, H-1B1, F-1, J-1, OPT, CPT or any other employment-based visa)


KPMG LLP and its affiliates and subsidiaries (“KPMG”) complies with all local/state regulations regarding displaying salary ranges. If required, the ranges displayed below or via the URL below are specifically for those potential hires who will work in the location(s) listed. Any offered salary is determined based on relevant factors such as applicant's skills, job responsibilities, prior relevant experience, certain degrees and certifications and market considerations. In addition, KPMG is proud to offer a comprehensive, competitive benefits package, with options designed to help you make the best decisions for yourself, your family, and your lifestyle. Available benefits are based on eligibility. Our Total Rewards package includes a variety of medical and dental plans, vision coverage, disability and life insurance, 401(k) plans, and a robust suite of personal well-being benefits to support your mental health. Depending on job classification, standard work hours, and years of service, KPMG provides Personal Time Off per fiscal year. Additionally, each year KPMG publishes a calendar of holidays to be observed during the year and provides eligible employees two breaks each year where employees will not be required to use Personal Time Off; one is at year end and the other is around the July 4th holiday. Additional details about our benefits can be found towards the bottom of our KPMG US Careers site at Benefits & How We Work.

Follow this link to obtain salary ranges by city outside of CA:

https://kpmg.com/us/en/how-we-work/pay-transparency.html/?id=8149_9_26

KPMG offers a comprehensive compensation and benefits package. KPMG is an equal opportunity employer. KPMG complies with all applicable federal, state and local laws regarding recruitment and hiring. All qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship status, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by applicable federal, state or local laws. The attached link contains further information regarding KPMG's compliance with federal, state and local recruitment and hiring laws. No phone calls or agencies please.

KPMG recruits on a rolling basis. Candidates are considered as they apply, until the opportunity is filled. Candidates are encouraged to apply expeditiously to any role(s) for which they are qualified that is also of interest to them.

Los Angeles County applicants: Material job duties for this position are listed above. Criminal history may have a direct, adverse, and negative relationship with some of the material job duties of this position. These include the duties and responsibilities listed above, as well as the abilities to adhere to company policies, exercise sound judgment, effectively manage stress and work safely and respectfully with others, exhibit trustworthiness, and safeguard business operations and company reputation. Pursuant to the California Fair Chance Act, Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, and San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
At CHAMP, we are redefining the technology used by Motor Vehicle Agencies and their stakeholders. We do so with innovative, cloud-based software that enhances efficiency, security, and interoperability. Our solutions replace outdated processes with modern, scalable platforms, enabling organizations to operate with greater speed and accuracy in an increasingly digital world.




Data Engineer

Location: Cleveland (Onsite – 4 days in office)

Department: IT Operations

Reports To: VP Product Engineering

Travel: <5%

Sponsorship: This position is not eligible for Visa Sponsorship

Relocation:This position is not eligible for Relocation




Your Impact

CHAMP is seeking a highly skilled and motivated Data Engineer to join our data management team. As a Data Engineer, you will be someone that combines elements of data analysis, data engineering, and data science using cloud-native technologies to support the extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) of data from various transactional sources into our Operational Data Store (ODS), Data Warehouse, and Data Marts. The ideal candidate will possess strong technical skills in SQL and Python for data manipulation, reporting, and visualization, along with a keen understanding of data architecture and database design.




We welcome candidates from all backgrounds. If you meet most of the qualifications below, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every single one.




What You’ll Do




SQL Query Development: Craft SQL queries for data reports based on specifications from both internal and external business stakeholders. Ensure queries are optimized for performance and accuracy, including standardization of queries. Create data visualizations and dashboards within a business intelligence tool based on these queries.
Data Report Generation: Use Python to develop scripts for automating the creation of data reports. This includes generating insightful graphs and charts to support decision-making processes.
ETL Process Management: Design and implement ETL processes that populate an ODS from transactional data stores, ensuring data integrity and consistency. Further, transform data from the ODS to a Data Warehouse, including normalization and standardization of data across disparate source systems. Leverage cloud services and custom scripts to create performant, resilient, and reliable ETL processes.
Data Warehouse Support: Participate in the creation and maintenance of a Data Warehouse and Data Marts. This includes writing SQL and Python code for data extraction, transformation, and loading tasks.
Data Documentation: Maintain comprehensive documentation of data definitions, ETL processes, and data architecture to support data warehouse and data mart development. Ensure documentation is clear and accessible to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Agile Team Leadership: Lead within the team to establish and continually improve team processes and procedures that ensure customer and company needs are satisfied effectively and efficiently.
Collaboration and Communication: Work closely with business and technical resources across the organization to ensure data needs are met and to foster a data-driven culture. Communicate complex data concepts and processes effectively to stakeholders with varying levels of technical expertise.




Requirements Qualifications

Education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, or a related field.
Experience: 2+ years of experience in a role involving SQL query writing, Python scripting for data processing, and ETL development.




Technical Skills:

Proficiency in SQL and experience with database management systems.
Strong Python programming skills, especially for data manipulation and visualization (e.g., Pandas, Matplotlib, Seaborn).
Experience with ETL tools and methodologies.
Understanding of data warehousing concepts and experience with data modeling.
Analytical Skills: Ability to analyze complex data sets to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences.







Why You’ll Like Working Here

Competitive Salary

Annual Bonus Potential

Health, Dental & Vision Insurance

Unlimited PTO Policy

Matching 401K with immediate vesting

Subsidized Lunches at our Cleveland Headquarters

Fast Paced work environment in a growth company

Newly Renovated Office Space




Salary Range

The expected salary range for this position is $125,000 - $145,000 annually. This is a good faith estimate, and final compensation may vary based on experience, skills, and geographic location.




We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We welcome applicants of all backgrounds, including veterans and individuals with disabilities. If you need accommodations during the application or interview process, please let us know. We’re here to help.




By joining CHAMP Titles, you will be an integral member of our team with many opportunities for personal growth and upward mobility in a growing organization. We have a great culture that is flexible, collaborative and welcoming.

Title: Jumeirah Opportunities - Mexico
Description:
About Jumeirah:

Jumeirah, a global leader in luxury hospitality and a member of Dubai Holding, operates an exceptional portfolio of 31 properties, including 33 signature F&B restaurants, across the Middle East, Europe, Asia and Africa. In 1999, Jumeirah changed the face of luxury hospitality with the opening of the iconic Jumeirah Burj Al Arab and the brand is now renowned worldwide for its distinguished beachfront resorts, esteemed city hotels and luxury residences. From the contemporary Maldivian island paradise at Jumeirah Olhahali Island to the art-inspired Jumeirah Capri Palace in Italy and the modern twist on a British classic at Jumeirah Carlton Tower in London, the brand has become synonymous with warm and generous service and the ability to craft distinctive and purposeful experiences that bring joy to guests from across the world.




About the Job:

An opportunity has arisen to join the Food & Beverage and Rooms Division teams at Jumeirah Beachfront properties in Dubai. We are hiring for the following positions:




Food & Beverage:

Waiter/Waitress
Senior Waiter/Waitress
Team Leader
Guest Relations Executive
Bartender
Senior Bartender




Rooms Division (Front Office, Concierge, Guest Relations):

Guest Services Executives
Team Leaders
Butlers




The main duties and responsibilities include:

Deliver exceptional, personalized service to all guests in accordance with Jumeirah standards across dining, reception, and concierge areas.
Anticipate guest needs and respond efficiently to inquiries, special requests, and service requirements.
Support daily operations within your assigned department, ensuring smooth coordination with colleagues and other departments.
Maintain high standards of cleanliness, organization, and presentation in all guest-facing areas.
Contribute to an outstanding guest experience by fostering a warm, welcoming atmosphere and promoting teamwork.




About you:

The ideal candidates for these positions will have the following experience and qualifications:

Minimum of 1–2 years of experience in the same role in a 5-star luxury hotel.
Strong communication and guest interaction skills, ability to remain calm and professional under pressure.
Proficient in English.
A passion for hospitality and delivering memorable guest experiences.




About the Benefits:

We offer an attractive tax-free salary, paid in Dirhams (AED), the local currency of the UAE. In addition, we offer generous F&B benefits, preferential hotel rates across our properties globally, excellent leave and health care package, flights home, accommodation, life insurance, functional incentives and other employee benefits making the role attractive to high performers and any applicant looking for a career with one of the most luxurious brands in the hospitality industry.
